compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: FIXED in 883cefcd. Added rebar._store.push.push_after_commit(tracker) and call it after the locked commit (lock released) in transition_compute (transition+reopen), claim_compute, compact_cli (gated: --skip-sync defers, so compact-on-close and compact-all batch their own push), compact_all_cli (one batch push), and delete_cli (main DELETE + re-invocation UNLINK-cleanup). Honors sync.push (off/async/always); best-effort (never fails the write). Reproduced before (claim/close/compact/delete left origin behind) and verified after (each leaves ahead=0). Tests: tests/interfaces/store/test_inline_write_push.py. Full offline 2113 + integration 20 green. Root cause was the tier-E bash->Python migration (e6e645ae/41217cc7/9f7bd246), NOT 0b32/7d1d.

compiled_state.description:
## Summary

The inline-commit write paths ÔÇö `transition`/`reopen`/`claim` (txn.py), `compact` (compact.py), and `delete` (delete.py) ÔÇö commit their events to the local `tickets` branch but **never call the auto-push**. Only the `append_event` family (`create`/`comment`/`edit`/`tag`/`untag`/`set_*` via `write_and_push`) and `link` (graph/_links.py) push. Because a push sends the whole `HEAD:tickets`, transition/compact/claim commits are normally *carried* to origin by the next push-capable write ÔÇö so the gap is invisible until such a write is the **last** operation of a session (e.g. closing an epic), which strands its commits as `PUSH_PENDING`.

This contradicts the documented contract in CLAUDE.md: 'Every write (create/edit/transition/claim/link/ÔÇŽ) auto-commits its event to the tickets branch AND auto-pushes to origin/tickets.'

## Reproduction Steps

In a fresh rebar repo with an `origin` remote:
1. `create` a ticket  Ôćĺ origin advances (push fires). ahead=0
2. `claim` it          Ôćĺ origin unchanged. ahead=1
3. `transition` closed Ôćĺ origin unchanged. ahead=3 (claim+status+compact)
4. `comment`           Ôćĺ origin advances, carrying all 4. ahead=0

Empirically reproduced deterministically in a clean repo (no concurrency, healthy origin), so it is a push **absence**, not a push **failure**.

## Expected vs Actual

- Expected: every write (incl. transition/claim/reopen/compact/delete) auto-pushes `HEAD:tickets` best-effort, per the CLAUDE.md contract.
- Actual: transition/claim/reopen/compact/delete commit but do not push; they rely on a later append_event write to carry them. A trailing status/compact strands commits (fsck Ôćĺ PUSH_PENDING).

## Root cause

The bash `write_commit_event` auto-pushed on EVERY write. The tier-E bashÔćĺPython migration (commits e6e645ae 'relocate transition/claim write core into rebar._commands.txn', 41217cc7 'port transition/reopen', 9f7bd246 'port compact/compact-all', ~2026-06-13) gave these paths their own inline rename+commit but omitted the push that write_and_push performs. NOT caused by 0b32 (1dbe5e7e, canonical bytes) or 7d1d (cee1fe98, HLC) ÔÇö those threaded canonical-bytes/HLC through txn/compact on 2026-06-17 but preserved the (already-missing) push wiring; pickaxe -S'push' on txn.py/compact.py/transition.py shows no push call was ever present or removed.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `transition`/`reopen`/`claim`/`compact`/`delete` trigger a best-effort push of `HEAD:tickets` after their locked commit (after lock release), honoring the `sync.push` policy (always/async/off).
- [ ] A transition/compact that is the LAST op of a session leaves origin/tickets current (no PUSH_PENDING in fsck).
- [ ] Push remains best-effort: a push failure never fails the write.
- [ ] No double-push regression for the append_event/link paths that already push.
- [ ] Test: in a repo with an origin, a lone `transition`/`claim`/`compact` (no following append_event write) leaves ahead=0.
compiled_state.title: Status/compact/claim/delete writes commit to tickets branch but never push (PUSH_PENDING when last op)

verify_commands.dd_text: each inline-commit write pushes on its own
